title: Event Dashboard
short_description: A live overview screen that gathers highlights, scores, media, and participation data into one dynamic event layer.
long_description: A real-time event dashboard that can display rankings, activity highlights, shared media, key metrics, and personalised outputs across one or multiple modules. It helps turn otherwise scattered interactions into one visible story for organisers, participants, and spectators. Ideal for conferences, activations, festivals, and exhibitions that want a stronger live overview and a more connected atmosphere.
visuals: Hero visual – Event Dashboard in use within a branded environment
Interface mockup – primary interaction flow for Event Dashboard
System overview – how visitors, screen, and backend connect
Scenario visual – themed use case of Event Dashboard

items.title: Core interaction flow
items.description: Base experience logic and visitor-facing interaction for Event Dashboard.

items.title: Branded interface skin
items.description: Adaptation of colours, typography, and visual language to the project context.

items.title: Basic analytics output
items.description: Tracking of key participation and usage signals relevant to the module.

items.title: Venue hardware outside agreed scope
items.description: Screens, sound, network, scenic build, mounts, printers, or special sensors are scoped separately unless explicitly listed.

items.title: Large-scale custom content production
items.description: Extensive editorial work, translations, media creation, or bespoke storytelling assets are separate services unless included.

items.title: Deep custom logic beyond the base module
items.description: Unique game systems, unusual integrations, or one-off backend behaviour require separate scoping and testing.

items.trigger: Visitor notices the installation or receives the entry prompt
items.experience: Event Dashboard communicates what to do and invites the first action.
items.feedback: Idle visuals, signage, motion, or ambient cue

items.experience: The main Event Dashboard experience unfolds and reacts to the visitor’s input.
items.feedback: Live visual, audio, or haptic response
items.configurable: Game rules, pacing, theme, interaction mode

items.trigger: Interaction reaches an outcome or end point
items.experience: Results, rewards, media, or next steps are revealed.
items.feedback: Score, confirmation, reward, save, or share prompt

items.title: Visual identity
items.description: Colours, fonts, screen assets, motion language, and tone can be adapted to brand or event theme.

items.title: Content layer
items.description: Texts, questions, media, products, scenes, or stories can be swapped to fit the context.

items.title: Interaction rules
items.description: Difficulty, duration, scoring, progression, and multiplayer logic can be tuned to the audience.

items.title: Power and connectivity
items.description: Stable power plus the right local network or internet setup depending on the module’s data and media needs.

items.title: Spatial planning
items.description: Sufficient visitor flow, visibility, and physical clearance for safe and intuitive use.

items.title: Display and input hardware
items.description: Screen, projector, sensors, touch surface, buttons, mobile flow, or other input devices according to the module setup.

items.title: Participation metrics
items.description: Starts, active users, completion rate, repeat plays, or dwell time depending on module behaviour.

items.title: Behaviour signals
items.description: Inputs, choices, timing, accuracy, usage paths, or content preferences that help interpret engagement quality.

items.title: Connected follow-up potential
items.description: Optional link to tokens, galleries, dashboards, exports, or post-event communication flows.

items.title: Visitor Token Pass
items.description: Links the module to identity, continuity, and personalised follow-up across touchpoints.

items.title: Shared Gallery / Media outputs
items.description: Useful when the module produces or benefits from captured media, highlights, or public recap moments.

items.title: User Experience & Interface Design
items.description: Refines the front-end quality and ensures the module feels coherent inside the wider BRDGE system.

items.title: Sponsor edition
items.description: Introduce branded rewards, visuals, or mechanics tied to a partner or campaign.

items.title: Tournament or leaderboard mode
items.description: Extend the base interaction into a competitive format across guests, teams, or time slots.

items.title: Token-connected continuity
items.description: Carry outcomes into another module, a personal recap, or a post-event reward flow.

items.title: Winter / festive edition
items.description: Re-skin the same interaction with seasonal visuals, story framing, and rewards.

items.title: Museum / education storyline
items.description: Adapt the module to teach content, reveal facts, or guide visitors through a knowledge-driven narrative.

items.title: Brand activation scenario
items.description: Translate the interaction into a product or campaign story that feels native to the brand world.

items.title: Needs a clearer participation hook
items.description: Event Dashboard gives visitors an immediate reason to engage instead of only observing.

items.title: Experience should feel more memorable
items.description: Event Dashboard creates a distinct interaction moment that people remember and talk about afterward.

items.title: Engagement should be measurable
items.description: Event Dashboard can capture interactions, completions, usage frequency, or connected outputs for reporting.
